description: Wan2.1 VACE 14B REFERENCE-TO-VIDEO / all-in-one editing. Same VACE graph as wan_vace_inpaint, but the reference_image input (node 162 -> WanVaceToVideo 49) is RE-CONNECTED: instead of cleaning the masked region away (removal), the masked region is regenerated to contain the SUBJECT from a reference image (object / character replacement, guided by the surrounding video + prompt). You give it a clip, a mask (where to insert), and a reference image (what to insert). Graph: ComfyUI core 'video_wan_vace_inpainting' with reference_image kept, derived for Spark by presets/wan_vace_ref2video.derive.py. Accelerated with the CausVid LoRA (4 steps, cfg 1).

about.what: Replaces a marked area of a video with the subject from a reference photo — VACE regenerates the masked region to contain your reference's object/character, blended into the scene's motion and lighting. (The removal-only sibling is wan_vace_inpaint.)
about.inputs: A video clip, a mask (white = the area to replace), and a reference image (the subject to put there).
about.key_knobs: Reference image — the subject to insert. Mask — where to insert it. Prompt — describe the combined result. CausVid strength — leave ~0.3-0.7 for fast 4-step renders.

_HOWITRUNS: No-build path (identical to wan_vace_inpaint): public yanwk image, comfy_run.py seeds ComfyUI, downloads the 4 weights + CausVid LoRA, converts the UI graph -> API via /object_info, applies patches (incl. placing the reference image onto node 162), and renders. All node types are CORE ComfyUI. ~42 GB cold start (use --idle-hold). --convert-only skips the model download.
_THREEINPUTS: This preset takes THREE files: the source clip (positional), the mask (--mask, white = area to replace, node 215), and the reference image (--face, the subject to insert, node 162). The launcher supports --mask and --face together. Example: `--preset wan_vace_ref2video shot.mp4 --mask area.png --face new_object.png --prompt '...'`.
_REFNOTE: VACE uses the reference as a SUBJECT/identity cue for the masked region, not a pixel-exact paste — it re-renders the area to match the clip's lighting/motion/perspective. A clean, well-lit reference of just the subject (plain background) works best. Mask resolution should match the source clip.
_PARAMS: Node ids come from the UI graph and survive conversion; patches target API format post-conversion. KSampler is preset for CausVid (4 steps, cfg 1); for the base model set --strength 0 --set 3.inputs.steps=20 --set 3.inputs.cfg=6.

ui.title: Wan2.1 VACE — reference-to-video (insert a reference subject into a masked area)

primary_input.label: Input video
primary_input.tooltip: The source clip. The masked region (white in the mask) is regenerated to contain the reference image's subject, blended into the clip.

secondary_input.label: Reference image (subject to insert)
secondary_input.tooltip: The subject/object to place into the masked region. A clean, well-lit reference on a plain background works best — VACE uses it as an identity cue and re-renders it to match the scene, not a pixel paste.

prompt.default: the reference subject naturally integrated into the scene, consistent lighting and perspective, seamless blending

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Describe the COMBINED result — the reference subject sitting in the scene, matching its lighting/perspective. Naming the surrounding context helps VACE blend it. (src: Wan VACE community guidance / ComfyUI docs)

negative.default: blur, ghosting, warping, flicker, seams, artifacts, mismatched lighting, floating object

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Discourage common compositing artifacts (seams, floating/mismatched subject).

ui.label: CausVid strength
ui.tooltip: CausVid acceleration LoRA strength. Keep it on (try 0.3-0.7) with Steps=4 / CFG=1 for fast renders. For the BASE model set this to 0, Steps ~20, CFG ~6.

ui.label: Steps
ui.tooltip: Sampler steps. CausVid on -> 4 is enough. Base model (strength 0) -> ~20.

ui.label: CFG
ui.tooltip: Guidance. CausVid needs CFG=1. Base model ~6.

ui.label: Width
ui.tooltip: Wan2.1 VACE 14B is a 480p-720p model; best at 1280x720 (or portrait). Divisible by 16. Match the source aspect; the mask must match these dims. (src: Wan2.1-VACE-14B docs)

ui.label: Height
ui.tooltip: 720p sweet spot (max ~1280); divisible by 16. (src: Wan2.1-VACE-14B docs)

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Wan frame count = 16*n+1 (81 ~= 5s at 16 fps). A mask must match the clip's frame count/res. (src: Wan-Video/Wan2.1)
